channel.title: World
channel.description: The Washington Post World section provides information and analysis of breaking world news stories. In addition to our world news and video, Post World News offers discussions and blogs on major international news and economic issues.

item.title: After quakes trap Venezuelans, the government impedes rescuers and aid
item.dc:creator: Helena Carpio, Terrence McCoy
item.description: In the nation’s gravest hour, the country’s authoritarian regime has crippled an effective response, say survivors, rescue workers and former officials.

item.title: Pope Leo plans to send a message about migrants on July Fourth
item.dc:creator: Anthony Faiola
item.description: The pope's visit to the Italian island of Lampedusa, desperate migrants' gateway to the West, will set up a split-screen moment as President Donald Trump holds an Independence Day rally.

item.title: As war stalls, Putin concedes he never cut a deal with Trump in Alaska
item.dc:creator: Mary Ilyushina
item.description: The Kremlin had framed the Anchorage summit as a turning point for the war in Ukraine. Now, Putin admits there was no deal, perhaps to get U.S. to help to broker a new one.

item.title: With little official help, Venezuelans in earthquakes rescue themselves
item.dc:creator: Helena Carpio
item.description: After two devastating quakes, first responders were fishermen, doctors, and emigres returning from abroad — a grassroots campaign filling a government void.
